steps.title: Plug in the lower bound of the training intensity
steps.content:
The recommended range is $0.5 \le p \le 0.85$. Substitute $p = 0.5$:
$$H = 140(0.5) + 55 = 70 + 55 = 125 \text{ bpm}$$

steps.title: Plug in the upper bound of the training intensity
steps.content:
Substitute $p = 0.85$:
$$H = 140(0.85) + 55 = 119 + 55 = 174 \text{ bpm}$$
Since the formula is linear with a positive slope, $H$ increases as $p$ increases, so the training heart rate range is $125 \le H \le 174$.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A: $125 \le H \le 174$ — The lower bound is 125 and the upper bound is 174, which matches our calculations. That is the correct answer.
  • B: Upper bound 125 — This would mean the maximum is 125, but 125 is the minimum training heart rate. Incorrect.
  • C: Upper bound 174 with a different lower bound — The lower bound shown is not 125, so this does not accurately represent the range. Incorrect.
  • D: Upper bound 104 — $H = 104$ corresponds to $p = 0.35$, which is below the recommended range. Incorrect.
